t_ch: 「当这个世界只剩下五分钟的时候，我只愿选择你，我只想望向你的眼睛。」
t_en: "When there are only five minutes left in this world, I only want to choose you, I only want to look into your eyes."

t_ch: 「你湿漉漉的棕黑色的眼睛——我多想干涸在你的眼底，即使要在绝望的告别中目送你远走，直至我化成粉末，消散在风中。」
t_en: "Your glistening brown-black eyes — how I would like to shrivel away beneath them, even if I have to see you go away in a desperate farewell, until I turn into powder and dissipate in the wind."

t_ch: 「我」最后到底有没有消散在风里？作为读者，你很在意这个故事是否烂尾。
t_en: Did "I" finally disappear like dust in the wind? As a reader, you care deeply about whether or not this story remains unfinished.

t_ch: 显然这不是终端机的密码。
t_en: This is evidently not the terminal's password.

t_ch: 一枚千纸鹤，轻轻一碰就有被撕碎的风险，你小心地将它拆开，字迹浮现于眼前——
t_en: An origami crane that risks falling apart with the slightest touch. You gently take it apart, and some words unfold before you...

t_ch: 「有时候我觉得我身体里蕴藏着一个热带雨林。」
t_en: "Sometimes I feel like I have a rainforest inside me."

t_ch: 「只要有你在场，就能触碰到你的掌心，雨林里的植物便会恣意生长，温暖包围着我。」
t_en: "As long as you are nearby and I can hold your palm, the plants in the rainforest will flourish and warmth will surround me."

t_ch: 「每次你要走的时候，热带雨林的河水决堤奔涌直落，雨水为何倾盆而下？或许是因为我的眼泪模糊了视线。」
t_en: "Why does the rain pour down every time you have to leave, when the river of the rainforest breaks its banks and rushes down? Maybe because my tears have blurred my vision."

t_ch: 只有单身太久的人才会胡思乱想，你毫不客气地裁定了作者的情感状况。
t_en: Only someone who's remained single for a long time could be so full of nonsense. You mercilessly determine the author's emotional state.

t_ch: 一枚千纸鹤，或许藏有终端机的密码，你小心地将它拆开，字迹浮现于眼前——
t_en: An origami crane that perhaps contains the password for this terminal. You gently take it apart, and some words unfold before you...

t_ch: 「如果不是他们的阻拦，我一定不会与你分别。」
t_en: "If they hadn't stopped me, I would never have parted with you."

t_ch: 「我只想找你作伴，将休息日填满。和你贴脸大头照，肥皂剧无聊也能一起笑。」
t_en: "I just want to have you as my companion to live my days off fully. We can laugh together, even if we are watching boring soap operas."

t_ch: 「我真的好喜欢你啊，佩佩，为了你我连磨牙饼干都买好了。」好像有哪里不对。
t_en: "I really like you, Peppy, I even bought some cookies for you to grind your teeth on." Something doesn't seem right.

t_ch: 你隐约记得佩佩是阿兰养的小狗，你不禁为空间站科员的精神状况感到担忧……
t_en: You dimly recall Peppy being Arlan's puppy, and can't help but worry about the mental state of the researchers aboard the space station...

t_ch: 显然这不是终端机的密码。
t_en: This is evidently not the terminal's password.

t_ch: 这枚千纸鹤虽无飞天神力，但却可以解决权限缺失问题。你迫不及待将它展开……
t_en: This origami crane doesn't have the power to fly, but it can solve your lack of access permissions. You can't wait to unfold it...

t_ch: 「哈哈，密码当然要简单才好啊！<unbreak>123</unbreak>，<unbreak>321</unbreak>，<unbreak>1234567</unbreak>！」
t_en: "Ha! Of course the password's gotta be simple, right? <unbreak>123</unbreak>, <unbreak>321</unbreak>, <unbreak>1234567</unbreak>!"

t_ch: 该设备已自动锁定，如需解锁请输入密码，密码！
t_en: The device is automatically locked. To unlock it, please enter your password. Password!

t_ch: （闭着眼乱输）
t_en: (Choosing numbers randomly with eyes closed)

t_ch: 密码错误，错误！如果忘记密码，请选择找回密码，找回！
t_en: Wrong password! If you forget your password, please select "Recover Password" to recover it!

t_ch: 如果只是路过，就不要随意使用该设备，设备！
t_en: Please don't attempt to use this device if you don't need access.

t_ch: 找回密码。
t_en: Recover password.

t_ch: 正在进行身份识别程序，程序！识别成功，确认为空间站科员！科员！
t_en: Verification protocol in progress. Verification Protocol! Verification successful, verified as space station researcher! Researcher!

t_ch: 离开
t_en: Leave

t_ch: 请选择找回方式：验证码或密保问题，问题！
t_en: Please choose retrieval method: Verification code or secret security question, question!

t_ch: 验证码
t_en: Verification code

t_ch: 警告！检测到该设备未绑定空间站终端设备，自动返回上一级，上一级。
t_en: Warning! This device was detected to not be linked to a space station terminal device. Automatically returning to previous stage, previous stage.

t_ch: 密保问题
t_en: Security question

t_ch: 正在查询权限等级，识别中…识别成功！成功！
t_en: Checking permission level, verification in progress... verification successful! Successful!

t_ch: 请回答问题——对您人生影响最大的人是？
t_en: Please answer the following question: Who has had the biggest influence on your life?

t_ch: 父母
t_en: Parents

t_ch: 回答错误，成年人应当自立，自立！
t_en: Incorrect answer, adults should be self-sufficient, and self-sufficient.

t_ch: 科室同事
t_en: Coworkers

t_ch: 回答错误，请不要自欺欺人，欺人！
t_en: Incorrect answer, please don't deceive yourself and others, don't deceive!

t_ch: 黑塔女士！
t_en: Madam Herta

t_ch: 正确的、中肯的、明智的、客观的、立体的、一针见血的！
t_en: Correct, impartial, wise, objective, 3-dimensional, and to the point!

t_ch: 黑塔女士最欣赏这种有思想有觉悟且敢于说实话的年轻科员！科员！
t_en: Madam Herta admires such thoughtful and conscious young researchers who dare to tell the truth! Dare to tell the truth!

t_ch: 请回答下一个问题——您在空间站取得的最优异的成果是？
t_en: Please answer the next question: What is the most outstanding research result you have achieved on the space station?

t_ch: 加入空间站
t_en: Joining the space station

t_ch: 错误！出道怎能即巅峰？简直毫无上进心，上进心！
t_en: Wrong! How can a debut be a peak achievement? There is no ambition, no ambition!

t_ch: α射线出现<unbreak>35</unbreak>号效应
t_en: Detecting type<unbreak>-35</unbreak> effects of alpha rays

t_ch: 错误！根本没有<unbreak>35</unbreak>号效应，请诚实作答，诚实！
t_en: Wrong! There is no such thing as a type<unbreak>-35</unbreak> effect for alpha rays. Please answer honestly, honestly!

t_ch: 暂无！
t_en: None so far

t_ch: 正确的、谦逊的、自我认识清晰的、勇于承认不足的！
t_en: A correct, humble, and self-aware answer that shows bravery to admit shortcomings!

t_ch: 黑塔女士最欣赏这种有思想有觉悟且敢于说实话的年轻科员！科员！
t_en: Madam Herta admires such thoughtful and conscious young researchers who dare to tell the truth! Dare to tell the truth!

t_ch: 请回答最后一个问题——本终端的备用实体密保卡存放于？
t_en: Please answer the last question: Where is the spare physical security card for this terminal stored?

t_ch: 「神奇马桶」的水箱里。
t_en: In the tank of the Magical Toilet.

t_ch: 错误！密保卡易溶于水！警惕，警惕！
t_en: Wrong! The security card is easily soluble in water! Must be careful, careful!

t_ch: 「飞天纸鹤」内藏玄机。
t_en: The mystery contained in the Flying Paper Crane.

t_ch: 正确正确！密保问题核验通过，通过！
t_en: Correct, correct! The security question has been verified, verified!

t_ch: 重启后再次激活该设备即可登录，登录！
t_en: Reboot and reactivate the device to log in, log in!

t_ch: 佩佩的狗牌上。
t_en: On Peppy's dog tag.

t_ch: 错误！工作时间禁止干扰其他同事！宠物也不例外！外！
t_en: Wrong! Interfering with colleagues during working hours is prohibited! Pets are no exception! No exception!

t_ch: 温馨提示：佩佩与空间站已签署正式员工合同。
t_en: Friendly Reminder: Peppy has signed a formal employment contract with the space station.

t_ch: 恭喜您已激活该设备，激活进度为：<unbreak>10%</unbreak>…<unbreak>20%</unbreak>…<unbreak>99%</unbreak>…哎呀！你访问的权限不见了！
t_en: Congratulations, device activated! Progress: <unbreak>10%</unbreak>... <unbreak>20%</unbreak>... <unbreak>99%</unbreak>... Ah! Please check your permissions!

t_ch: 正在识别用户信息…识别成功，已为您解除备用装置的光学迷彩。
t_en: Identifying user information... identified. Backup device decloaking successful.

t_ch: 请在该终端附近<color=#dbc291ff>寻找「飞天纸鹤」</color>，以解决权限缺失问题。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Find an Origami Crane</color> near this terminal to solve your lack of credentials.

t_ch: 设备激活进度已达<unbreak>99%</unbreak>，请输入最终密码破解权限！
t_en: Device activation progress has reached <unbreak>99%</unbreak>, please enter the final password to unlock access!

t_ch: <unbreak>1233211234567</unbreak>
t_en: <unbreak>1233211234567</unbreak>

t_ch: 设备屏幕陡然一黑，随后出现了你熟悉的开机页面。然而你心中并无欣喜，只有繁琐流程带来的无尽疲惫。
t_en: The device screen turns stark black, followed by the familiar startup page. However, your heart holds no joy — only the endless exhaustion from following complicated procedures.

t_ch: <unbreak>27149</unbreak>
t_en: <unbreak>27149</unbreak>

t_ch: 你想起旧时代影片中流传出来的神秘数字，它被称为是「宇宙的密码」，也是神秘档案的编号。它背后的故事让你双手颤抖。
t_en: You recall a string of mysterious digits that were mentioned in a certain old-era film. It was known as the Code of the Universe, and was also the number of a certain cryptic file. Its backstory causes your hands to tremble, just thinking about it.

t_ch: 你输入后，设备上跳出一行冰冷的红字：<color=#eb4d3dff>「密码错误，请重新输入正确密码。」</color>
t_en: You enter it, but all that pops up on the device is a line of cold red text: <color=#eb4d3dff>"Password error. Please enter the correct password."</color>

t_ch: <unbreak>9527</unbreak>
t_en: <unbreak>9527</unbreak>

t_ch: 「<unbreak>9527</unbreak>」，亲切且饱含力量的数字，它曾见证过古早的数据传输时代的辉煌，在它背后埋葬了无数过客的青春。你想了想，决定试试这串数字。
t_en: "<unbreak>9527</unbreak>," a kind and powerful string of digits. It has witnessed the glory of the ancient data transmission era, and behind those digits are buried the youthful days of countless many. After mulling it over, you decide to give it a try.

t_ch: 你输入后，设备上只跳出一行冰冷的红字：<color=#eb4d3dff>「密码错误，请重新输入正确密码。」</color>
t_en: You enter it, but all that pops up on the device is a line of cold red text: <color=#eb4d3dff>"Password error. Please enter the correct password."</color>

t_ch: 密码输入正确，登陆中…登陆成功！
t_en: Password entered correctly, login in progress... Login successful!

t_ch: 恭喜你进入了没有任何文件的虚拟荒原！
t_en: Congratulations! You have now entered a virtual wasteland devoid of any documents!

t_ch: 屏幕显示：「预约会议」。字符下方有详细的表格，对应着不同的时间段。
t_en: The screen display reads "Schedule a Meeting." There are detailed tables below with corresponding times.

t_ch: 其他会议室都已被订满，只有这间还空着。
t_en: All the other meeting rooms are fully booked. This is the only free room.

s_en: Male Researcher of the Department of Insight
t_ch: 大家肯来这么危险的地方开会，已经给足你们科室面子了！
t_en: The fact that everyone's still willing to meet up in such a dangerous place is a glowing endorsement for your department!

t_ch: 你望着空白的时间段，不知为何手忽然有点痒——
t_en: You look for a blank spot, and your hands suddenly begin to feel itchy...

t_ch: 预约会议。
t_en: Book a meeting.

t_ch: 选定时间后，页面弹出了窗口，要求勾选「参会人」，你没有找到退出按钮。
t_en: After selecting your time, an interface pops up asking you to select Participants. You cannot find the Exit button.

t_ch: 你决定从科员名单里随意勾选几个，哪怕不是一个科室也没有关系，毕竟跨领域的探索才是未来的研究趋势！
t_en: You decide to randomly check a few names from the faculty list, not even caring that they are not from the same department. After all, interdepartmental research is the future of academia!

t_ch: 屏幕上弹出一行大字：「预约成功！」。
t_en: A row of characters pops up on the screen: "Booking complete!"

t_ch: 离会议开始的时间尚早，先去其他地方转转吧。
t_en: It's still too soon for the meeting to start, so let's take a stroll elsewhere.

t_ch: 会议室的人们正吵个不停，你甚至听不清每个人到底在说什么。
t_en: The people in the conference room are so noisy that you can't even hear what anyone's saying.

s_en: Male Researcher of the Department of Insight
t_ch: 结果呢？你拿出来的那套方案压根不适用于我们科室！你压根就没考虑过我们的难处！
t_en: So? Your solution is in no way appropriate for our department! You've never even given a single thought to our concerns!

s_en: Female Researcher of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 你懂得多咯，你来做个适用的方案咯？
t_en: Since you know so much, then do you have a plan that is suitable?

s_en: Male Researcher of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 这都已经是「第<unbreak>12</unbreak>版」的方案了……
t_en: We're already at "Version <unbreak>12</unbreak>" of the plan...

s_en: Male Researcher of the Department of Insight
t_ch: 你以为是我故意的啊？还不是你们之前提的都不行啊！这该怎么办？
t_en: Do you think that's my fault? Or are we supposed to ignore all of your previous poor suggestions!? What are we going to do about it now!?

s_en: Female Researcher Department of Cosmic Geography
t_ch: 炖蛋？什么东西要拿来炖蛋？
t_en: Egg custard? What do you need egg custard for?

s_en: Male Researcher of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 下一版我改…嗯，一定改……
t_en: I'll revise that part in the next version... I'll definitely revise it...

s_en: Female Researcher of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 我认为，无尽的修改和返工，就是科研进程中最大的败笔。
t_en: You know what I think? Endless revision and doing the same work over and over again is the biggest failure of the research process!

s_en: Female Researcher Department of Cosmic Geography
t_ch: 啊？我没有提这个修改需求的呀，你们不要乱讲哦。
t_en: What are you looking at me for? This revision business wasn't my idea.

t_ch: 这场争执已经没必要再听下去，因为会议中缺失关键的「调停者」。而你意识到，这将是会议发起人应该承担的责任——
t_en: There's no need to keep listening to this debate go back and forth because the key "mediator" is missing. And you realize that the organizer of the meeting assumes this responsibility...

t_ch: 你大步走上前，开始了极致的临场演说
t_en: You stride forward, and begin delivering the ultimate impromptu speech.

t_ch: 「我看第1版就挺好的呀？你们自己看是不是符合现在的全部需求？」
t_en: "I think the Version 1 is quite satisfactory. You all can see very well for yourselves whether it meets all the current requirements."

t_ch: 纷争止息，正确答案近在眼前，但大家往往会忽视它。你正是抓住了这个思维盲区，才能顺利破题。
t_en: The argument abates and the correct answer is within grasp, but everyone seems to ignore it. It is precisely by grasping this blind spot that you're able to successfully crack it.

t_ch: 会议室里忽然安静了起来，在场的众人被<color=#dbc291ff>「第1版」</color>拯救，而你也体验到了助人为乐的快乐。
t_en: The conference room draws quiet, and everyone present is roundly saved by <color=#dbc291ff>Version 1</color>. You also get to experience the pleasure of helping others.

t_ch: 桌面上的电子屏显示着清晰的字符：「该会议室预约已满，请更换会议室」。
t_en: The display on the desktop clearly reads: "This conference room has been booked out. Please try another room."

t_ch: 你有些懊恼，觉得好像失去了什么…但回想起那次开会经历，你已经领悟了开会的精髓。
t_en: You are a little chagrined and feel as if you have lost something. But then you think back to the experience of that meeting before and feel like you've already grasped the essence of meetings.

t_ch: 音频播放器的界面很特殊，你阅读着旁边的文字：「原创音乐大赛已进入海选打榜投票阶段，得票数最高的选手将有机会获得晋级直通车！」
t_en: The speaker's interface is quite special, and you read the text beside it: "The Original Music Singing Contest has entered the audition voting stage. The contestant with the highest number of votes will have a chance to enjoy a direct promotion!"

t_ch: 目前排名第一的歌曲名叫：《黑塔女士乃世界之光》，第二名叫：《黑塔情歌》，第三名叫：《黑塔女士发现我一个人做实验》。
t_en: The current #1 song is titled "Madam Herta is the Light of the World." #2 is "Herta's Love Song," and #3 is "Busted by Madam Herta Experimenting Alone."

t_ch: 被千篇一律的「黑塔女士」系列曲踩在脚底的那首音乐，反倒是吸引了你的注意力。
t_en: There is one track, flooded over by cliché Madam Herta worship tracks, that actually ends up piquing your attention.

t_ch: 播放这首《危机预知》。
t_en: You decide to play that song: "Crises."

t_ch: 音乐的洪流倾泻而出，你闭上眼睛欣赏，心神好似遨游到宇宙尽头。
t_en: A torrent of music flows out. You close your eyes off to the world and as you enjoy it, your mind seems to swim off to the edge of the universe.

t_ch: 为了这首歌，你决定…
t_en: For this song, you decide...

t_ch: 这首歌才代表了真正的音乐艺术，可是它竟然排在最后，你怎能接受这位富有才华的选手就这样被埋没！
t_en: This performance represents the true art of music, and yet it was at the bottom of the list. How can you accept that such a talented contestant was just buried away?

t_ch: 为它打榜1次
t_en: Why not give them an upvote?

t_ch: 你选择了「打榜」，然而它的排名却依然垫底。你注意到，原来大家的票数已高达几十票，你的一票看来不会改变它被埋没的宿命。
t_en: You choose to upvote, but it's still ranked near the bottom. That's when you notice that the other contestants already have a few dozen votes or so. It's no wonder a single vote won't change this song's destiny of being buried in the bottom rank.

t_ch: 为它打榜<unbreak>10</unbreak>次
t_en: Why not give it ten upvotes?

t_ch: 你注意到，《危机预知》在榜单上从垫底跃升到页面中部，但如果想让它传播到更多人的耳朵里，似乎还差得远呢。
t_en: You notice that "Crises" has leaped up from the bottom to the middle of the leaderboard, but it still seems like a tall order to spread it on to even more ears.

t_ch: 无意义的歌词与噪音般的旋律令人恶心，但它们霸占大众的耳朵实在太久…是时候让音乐市场知道什么才是真正的音乐了！你决定——
t_en: Meaningless lyrics and noisy melodies are horrid, but they've been in the public's ears and minds for too long... It's time to let the music scene know what real music is all about! You decide...

t_ch: 为它打榜<unbreak>100</unbreak>次
t_en: Why not give them <unbreak>100</unbreak> upvotes?

t_ch: 你感到手指关节有些隐隐作痛，这或许就是「打榜」的副作用吧。这首歌的排名一跃而上，从末端飞向了顶部，甚至力压了《黑塔女士乃世界之光》一票。
t_en: You feel a vague pain in your finger joints, which is probably a side effect of clicking "Upvote" so many times. The song jumps up in the rankings, flying from the bottom to the top, even edging out "Madam Herta is the Light of the World" by one vote.

t_ch: 虽然自己的手指关节或许要歇上三两天才会恢复，但这短暂的疼痛却成就了一名真正的艺术家！
t_en: Your knuckles may need two or three days of rest to recover, but this temporary pain has brought a true artist to fame!

t_ch: 算了，不打榜了。
t_en: Forget it, I won't upvote.

t_ch: 你仔细地观察了下这份榜单，你发现它只在空间站内流通，你决定就此停手。
t_en: You carefully look over the leaderboard, and discover that it only circulates around the space station — so you decide to stop.

t_ch: 算了，不打榜了。
t_en: Forget it, I won't upvote.

t_ch: 你研读榜单规则许久，发现第一名的奖励「黑塔女士签名照」毫无含金量，就算拿到第一也没什么意义，你悻悻收回手。
t_en: You pore over the leaderboard rules and discover that the first prize — A Signed Photo of Madam Herta — is worthless. Even if you won, it would be meaningless. You resentfully withdraw your hand.

t_ch: 还是别帮它打榜了。
t_en: Just don't upvote it.

t_ch: 眼见榜单上全是些毫无营养、只知道吹捧黑塔的歌曲与评论。你认为点开这个页面简直是脏了你的眼。
t_en: You come to realize that the whole leaderboard is just spam promoting songs and comments about Madam Herta. You begin to feel dirty merely opening up the page.

t_ch: 你正打算离开，屏幕上却突然出现了一个弹窗…上面写着<color=#dbc291ff>「粉丝奖励」</color>。
t_en: Just as you are about to leave, a pop-up window appears on the display... It says <color=#dbc291ff>"Fan Reward."</color>

t_ch: 传来了奇怪的声音……
t_en: There's a strange voice...

t_ch: 奇怪的声音不断重复，你追溯声源，仔细聆听——
t_en: The strange voice is saying something over and over. You trace it back to the source and listen carefully...

t_ch: 这声音似乎蕴藏着某种深意，但除了疑似「王」的发音外，你听不懂它在说些什么。空间站里有能翻译它的工具吗？
t_en: This voice seems to contain some kind of poignant meaning, but all you can make out is the word "King." Are there tools aboard the space station to translate it?

t_ch: 你记得在空间站的某处记载着奇物「不可承受之重」的神奇功效，它能让人迅速掌握一门语言。经过比对，眼前这顶王冠正是神奇的「不可承受之重」。
t_en: You recall a record in the space station about how the Curio "Unbearable Weight" can instantly make someone fluent in a language. After careful scrutiny, you conclude this crown before you is none other than the wondrous Unbearable Weight.

t_ch: 学习「怪声」的语言。
t_en: Learn the language the strange voice is speaking in

t_ch: 宝贵的学习机会岂能浪费。
t_en: Valuable learning opportunities cannot be squandered.

t_ch: 倒也不必这么谨慎，在博识学会重现了以利亚萨拉斯的联觉信标技术之后，宇宙中已经不再有语言的障碍了。有机会的时候就好好使用它吧。
t_en: There's no need to be so cautious. Language barriers are a thing of the past ever since the Intelligentsia Guild recreated Elias Salas' Synesthesia Beacon technology. Use it when you have the chance.

t_ch: 崭新的语言涌入你的脑海。你只觉得头昏脑涨，头顶像是压有千斤巨石……
t_en: A completely new language floods your synapses. You feel dizzy, and a huge boulder seems to be weighing down on your head...

t_ch: 恭喜，你学会了一门全新的语言！
t_en: Congratulations — you've learned a whole new language!

t_ch: 现在你总算明白这件奇物名字的由来了。取名的人一定和你一样，头顶承受了太多不该有的压力……
t_en: Now you finally understand the reason behind this Curio's name. Whosoever named it must've also experienced this same crushing weight upon their head...

t_ch: 那怪声的意义在你耳中变得清晰起来——「而你，将加冕为约特伍德的帝皇……」
t_en: The meaning of the strange voice became clear inside your head: "And you shall be crowned Emperor of the Tottonids..."

t_ch: 尽管你和约特伍德人素未谋面，但你相信它们一定是这宇宙里最具慧眼的族群。
t_en: Despite never having met the Tottonids, you trust they must be in possession of the greatest taste in all the universe.

t_ch: 你注意到这里贴着一张泛黄的留言条：「谁能听懂它在说什么？我权限不够，借不到『不可承受之重』……」
t_en: You notice a yellowed note that reads, "Can anyone understand what it is saying? My authorization isn't high enough, so I can't borrow the Unbearable Weight..."

t_ch: 你回到了这里，故事开始的地方。恍惚之间，思绪开始飞旋——
t_en: You're back where the story started. In a trance, your thoughts begin to whirl —

t_ch: 你想起一些匆匆别过的面孔与名字，他们在同行的旅途中渐渐消逝……
t_en: You remember faces and names that you passed by, gradually disappearing as you journey together...

t_ch: 你想起了自己曾做出的选择，你至今也无法确定它们是否会令你感到后悔……
t_en: You remember the choices you made, and how you still have no idea whether or not you will come to regret them...

t_ch: 该踏上新的旅途了。在不远的将来，你坚信自己能找到所有问题的答案。
t_en: It's time to embark on a new journey. You are confident you'll find answers to all of your questions in the not-too-distant future.

t_ch: 应该在这附近吧？
t_en: They should be nearby, right?

t_ch: 可我没听到声音呢……
t_en: But I can't hear anything...

t_ch: 除开机械运作的声音与少许人员交谈的声音，你好像还听见了别的动静……
t_en: Besides the sounds of machinery and a few voices chatting, you seem to hear something else...

t_ch: 浇浇我呗，浇浇我呗！
t_en: Water me~ Water me!

t_ch: 浇浇我呗，浇浇我呗！
t_en: Water me~ Water me!

t_ch: 声音来自这株…植物？你侧耳屏息，等待它下一次「开口」。
t_en: The voice is coming from this... plant? You hold your breath, waiting for it to "speak" again.

t_ch: 哎哟哟，你就是我命中注定的那个人吗？
t_en: Ohhh, are you the chosen one for me?

t_ch: （沉默不语）
t_en: (Silence)

t_ch: 喂！你明显听到我说话了！别装！
t_en: Hey! You clearly heard me! Quit pretending!

t_ch: （庄重点头）
t_en: (Nodding gravely)

t_ch: 很好，看来你已经做好了觉悟，太好了……
t_en: Good, looks like you've understood me. That's great...

t_ch: 终于有人能听懂我的话了！
t_en: Finally, someone who can understand what I'm saying!

t_ch: 你为什么会说话？
t_en: Why are you able to talk?

t_ch: 在这么个千奇百怪的世界里，这重要吗？
t_en: With all the craziness out there in this world, is that really important?

t_ch: 我好像还没睡醒…
t_en: Am I dreaming...

t_ch: 你很清醒！
t_en: You're wide awake!

t_ch: 是这样，我脚下这块土壤缺水缺得厉害。经过那次「大动静」之后，液滴系统罢工了。
t_en: That's right. This soil beneath me is bone dry, and after that "big incident," the sprinklers stopped working.

t_ch: 植物需要水，就像人需要吃饭一样。请你找点水来浇浇我吧！不然我会枯萎的……
t_en: Plants need water just as people need food to survive. Please, can you find me some water? I'll wither and die otherwise...

t_ch: 让我找找看…
t_en: Let me have a look...

t_ch: 千万别用什么刺激性的液体！那样会让我直接「化作春泥」的！
t_en: And please, none of that stimulating drink! That muddy liquid would just make me rot!

t_ch: 咖啡？
t_en: Coffee?

t_ch: 既然植物都可以说话，那为什么不可以喝咖啡呢？你思来想去，决定让它品尝一下这份苦涩的美味。
t_en: If plants can talk, why can't they drink coffee too? You think it over before deciding to let it sample some of this beautiful bitter beverage.

t_ch: 啊停停停！别伤害我！要是喝了这个，空间站就会缺少一株正直善良并拥有独立思考意识的植物啦！
t_en: Ah, stop stop stop! Don't hurt me! If I drink this, the space station will no longer have an honest, kind, and conscious plant!

t_ch: 卡利白。
t_en: Kapo-Kali.

t_ch: 不对不对！这个东西的颜色一看就不是什么普通液体！品尝新饮料的好意我心领了，只要给我最普通的纯净水就行。
t_en: No no no! This thing is not a normal color! I appreciate your offer to broaden my tastes, but the simplest pure water will do.

t_ch: 纯净水。
t_en: Pure Water.

t_ch: 你将纯净水顺着植物的根部倒入，干涩的土壤瞬间将它们全部吸收。
t_en: You pour pure water down near the roots of the plant, and the dry soil instantly absorbs it all.

t_ch: 啊——嗝！舒服！痛快！好久没喝得这么爽啦！
t_en: Ah... *hiccup*! Ahhh~ Now that's good! I haven't had such a good drink for a very long time!

t_ch: 我虽没有手脚，但还有颗感恩之心。刚喝下去的水还没彻底吸收，没法给你报酬。<color=#dbc291ff>明天</color>再来吧，肯定给你好果子吃！
t_en: I may not have any limbs, but I still have a grateful heart that beats its thanks. I can't give you the fruits of your kindness just yet, but come back <color=#dbc291ff>tomorrow</color> and I'll have something fresh for you!

t_ch: 之后再说。
t_en: Maybe later.

t_ch: 你可别糊弄我啊，我就在这儿等你，一动不动！
t_en: Can't trick me — I'll be waiting for you right here. I'm not going anywhere else!

t_ch: 你可算来了，知不知道昨天你走了后，我这儿路过了多少科员？那叫一个数不胜数啊！我听到了好多小道消息！谁都不把我当外人，一个劲儿在我面前讲。
t_en: Can you guess how many researchers passed me by yesterday after you left? Way too many to count! I got to hear tons of gossip. I'm an insider for everyone's secret conversations.

t_ch: 哎哟，那叫一个刺激劲爆！这空间站看着人不是很多，但这人与人之间的事儿啊…那叫一个，五彩缤纷！
t_en: Wow, now that's a blast! This space station doesn't seem to have many people aboard, but the human interactions are... well, ferociously intense!

t_ch: 你还挺八卦。
t_en: I didn't take you to be a gossiper.

t_ch: 我又不能走动，除了听人类说话，也没别的事儿干呀。
t_en: I can't even walk, so I've got nothing better to do than listen to humans chatting.

t_ch: 你耳朵真灵。
t_en: Your ears must be really good.

t_ch: 还行还行，那些家伙说话也大声。不过喝了水之后确实感觉听力水平恢复了不少。
t_en: They're alright. Those guys were talking pretty loudly, but in any case — all that water has definitely helped my hearing recover.

t_ch: 你精神多了。
t_en: You're doing much better now.

t_ch: 那可不？喝了水，皮肤没褶子了，头发都变回嫩绿色了！自然精气神就好了。
t_en: Don't you think? With that cool drink, my skin is feeling wrinkle-free and my hair's already turned green again! Natural energy is the best.

t_ch: 总之，多谢你昨天的帮助！喏，这是我许诺给你的东西，别客气，拿着吧！以后没事儿多来看看我，万一又停水呢。
t_en: Anyway, thanks for your help yesterday! Well, here's what I promised you. Go on, take it! Come back and check up on me later, just in case the water goes out again.

t_ch: 空间站真是鱼龙混杂，居然有个假面愚者混在科员里，想搞点大事。
t_en: This space station really is a mixed bag. There's even a Masked Fool in disguise among the researchers, trying to cook up something big.

t_ch: 空间站真是鱼龙混杂，你知道前阵子保卫科抓住了一个企图修改程序语法的谜语人吗？
t_en: This space station really is a mixed bag. Did you know the security team caught a Riddler trying to edit the system syntax?

t_ch: 空间站真是鱼龙混杂，听说有个叫温世齐的，偷偷拿黑塔的终端算力挖琥珀币。
t_en: This space station really is a mixed bag. I heard there's a guy named Wen Shiqi who secretly uses Herta's terminal to mine Amber coins.

t_ch: 空间站真是鱼龙混杂，听说前阵子星穹列车又来了，里面有个叫丹恒的小哥长挺帅，有几个科员在打赌谁能追到他呢。
t_en: This space station really is a mixed bag. I heard the Astral Express stopped by recently with a handsome passenger named Dan Heng — and all the researchers were betting on who could score him.

t_ch: 空间站真是鱼龙混杂，那天甚至有个虚卒从我面前跑了过去……
t_en: This space station really is a mixed bag. A few days back, there was even a Voidranger running past me...

t_ch: 空间站真是鱼龙混杂，听说机械贵族螺丝咕姆过阵子要来访问这里哩。
t_en: This space station really is a mixed bag. I heard the mech aristocrat Screwllum is going to pay us a visit soon.

t_ch: 空间站真是鱼龙混杂，可怎么就没有第二株会说话的植物陪陪我呢。
t_en: This space station really is a mixed bag. How do we not have any other talking plants to keep me company?

t_ch: 空间站真是鱼龙混杂，我有一次好像听见墙壁在跟我说话。
t_en: This space station really is a mixed bag. I thought I could hear the walls talking to me once.

t_ch: 路过此处时，你瞥见几本书摆放在一起，从光洁的封皮上看，像是那种填充书柜的大部头典籍，缺乏翻阅的痕迹。
t_en: You glimpse several books together as you pass by. Judging by the glossy covers, they seem to be the kind of thick space-fillers used to make a bookshelf look impressive. They show no signs of having actually been read.

t_ch: 翻翻书柜里有什么。
t_en: Browse the bookshelf to see what's there.

t_ch: 一套《星神的失落——银河民俗考》丛书，一本《社会生活中的星神崇拜印痕》，《银河神话举隅：神话中的星神内涵叙略》，以及《星神之下：作为意志与表象的低维世界》。
t_en: There's a copy of The Fall of The Aeons – A Discussion on Cosmic Traditions series, a copy of Marks of Aeon Worship in Society, a copy of Cases of Cosmic Myths: The Meanings Behind Aeons in Myth, and a copy of Beneath the Aeons: The Low-Dimensional World As A Will and A Surface Phenomenon.

t_ch: 随手抽一本书检查。
t_en: Pick out a book at random and flip through.

t_ch: 指尖划过书脊，最终在《星神之下：作为意志与表象的低维世界》的封皮上停下。
t_en: Your fingertips run over the book spines before landing on Beneath the Aeons: The Low-Dimensional World As A Will and A Surface Phenomenon.

t_ch: 书籍被包上了空间站标识的封皮，内里却都是货真价实的文献巨作。你翻阅书籍时，一张纸条从书页间掉了出来。
t_en: The book is bound with covers containing the logo of the space station, but the contents are all authentic masterpieces. As you flip through the pages, a piece of paper falls out.

t_ch: 纸条上写满了看起来潦草的字，你努力辨认，发现页面印着「密卷科」字样，右下角还贴上了猫咪图案的贴纸。
t_en: The paper is filled with scribbled words. Squinting to make things out, you discover the words "Department of Insight" printed on the page. There's also a cat-shaped sticker at the bottom-right corner.

t_ch: 你猜测这张纸条或许来自某位密卷科科员的笔记本。
t_en: You surmise that this note could be from a Department of Insight researcher's notebook.

t_ch: 又见到你了…除了星神…我了解的知识并不多……
t_en: We meet again... I don't know much about anything apart from the Aeons...

t_ch: 你有什么事情…吗？
t_en: How... can I help you?

t_ch: 我捡到一张纸条，是你的吗？
t_en: I found this note. Is it yours?

t_ch: 是…是在《星神之下：作为意志与表象的低维世界》里夹在的那张纸吗？
t_en: W—Was it inside the book called Beneath the Aeons: The Low-Dimensional World As A Will and A Surface Phenomenon?

t_ch: 是我的！那不是笔记啦，那是…是我特地留下的实验品。成功了，没想到实验真的成功了！！
t_en: It's mine! It's not a note, but... an experiment specifically placed there. It worked. I didn't expect it to be a success!

t_ch: 太好了…我对星神的研究与信念，终于得到了回应…呜呜！
t_en: Great... my research and faith in the Aeons have finally yielded a response... Woohoo!

t_ch: 「实验」？什么实验？
t_en: An "experiment"? What experiment?

t_ch: 「回应」？什么回应？
t_en: A "response"? What response?

t_ch: 不是吧，你在书里找到的纸条，就不看看那本书讲什么吗？
t_en: Come on. Didn't you even read the book you found the note in?

t_ch: 《星神之下：作为意志与表象的低维世界》说的是我们处在一个低维世界里，感应星神的存在时，可能遇到的是一种更高维度的投影。
t_en: Beneath the Aeons: The Low-Dimensional World As A Will and A Surface Phenomenon talks about us living in a low-dimensional world, and the Aeons we sense are simply projections from a higher dimension.

t_ch: 我留下纸条，祈求星神投影现身，果然把你给「召唤」来了。
t_en: The note I left is a prayer for the Aeons to project and materialize and, as expected, you were summoned.

t_ch: 说吧！你是不是来自一个迥然不同的世界？你是不是生活在星神的「高维世界」里？
t_en: Tell me! Are you from a radically different world? Do you come from the higher dimensional world of the Aeons?

t_ch: 什么乱七八糟的…
t_en: What the heck are you harping on about...

t_ch: 你去医疗科查查吧？
t_en: Do you need to get a medical check-up?

t_ch: 别急，听我给你分析，你要多留意那些看似不起眼的细节。
t_en: Don't worry. Just listen to my analysis. You have to pay attention to those seemingly inconspicuous details.

t_ch: 比如说…你知道公司的取木星吗？把它们出口的木材，按「公司」采购清单上的种类名称排序，摘出开头第一个读音，再拼起来——
t_en: Like... you know the IPC's planet, Junipter? Take the types of lumber it exports, list them according to the categories in the IPC's purchase orders, then take the first letter of each one and spell it out —

t_ch: 「灰——咪——」你听，发音是不是很像「毁灭」？这就是「毁灭」星神在背后干扰「公司」运转的证据！
t_en: "D... E... S... T..." see? Doesn't it spell out the start of "Destruction"? This is proof that the Aeon of Destruction is secretly pulling all the strings in the IPC!

t_ch: 你这就是瞎编啊。
t_en: You're just making this all up.

t_ch: 哎，我这是建立在扎实文献研究之上的合理推论！
t_en: Hey, it's a reasonable conclusion to reach based on solid research of the literature!

t_ch: 这发音哪里像了？
t_en: How is that related?

t_ch: 要用心去感受，仔细听听，不像吗？
t_en: You have to feel it with your heart and focus. Are you getting it?

t_ch: 多读点文献吧！希望你能抛开愚昧无知的陈腐思想，积极拥抱科学的前沿理论！
t_en: Keep on reading, then! I hope you can put aside your foolish superstitions and embrace the theories of modern science!

t_ch: 算了，你开心就好。
t_en: Whatever. So long as you're happy.

t_ch: …我懒得和你说话。
t_en: ... I don't wanna chat with you right now.

t_ch: 你也别气馁，我能有这样的研究发现，是因为在密卷科打下了扎实的理论基础，和你的起点是不一样的……
t_en: Don't lose hope. I owe this scientific discovery to the theoretical foundation given to me at the Department of Insight. We come from different backgrounds...

t_ch: 唉，我看你确实也不像高维存在。我还要忙，回头再聊吧！
t_en: Ack, I don't think you really look like a high-dimensional being. I've gotta get back to work. Chat with you later then!

t_ch: 你依稀记得，在主控舱段时常见到密卷科科员们的身影，或许该去那里打听打听。
t_en: You vaguely remember seeing the members of the Department of Insight flitting around the master control zone. Maybe you can go there and ask around.

t_ch: 这杯咖啡静默地坐在桌上，好似一名优雅的贵族，即便杯身冰冷，它依旧昂着高傲的头颅——散发馥郁的浓香。
t_en: This cup of coffee sits silently on the table, like an elegant aristocrat. Despite being cold, it holds its head aloft and exudes a rich aroma.

t_ch: 这股香味仿佛有种魔力，望着深褐色的液体，你的思绪似乎也坠入了深渊……
t_en: This aroma seems to have its own magic and as you gaze into the deep brown liquid, you feel your thoughts slipping into the abyss...

t_ch: 浅尝一口。
t_en: Take a sip.

t_ch: 嘴唇触碰杯身的刹那，你感到些许余温。或许这杯咖啡从被制作出来到现在，并未过去太久，仍带有一种「初生」的美感。
t_en: You feel some lingering warmth the moment your lips touch the rim of the cup. Perhaps it hasn't been long since this coffee was brewed, and it still retains a kind of "newborn" beauty.

t_ch: 只可惜你只浅尝了一口，并未品出更多的韵味。也许应该再多喝一些！
t_en: It's a shame you only took a tiny sip and couldn't taste more of the aroma. Maybe you should drink some more!

t_ch: 再浅尝一口。
t_en: Take another sip.

t_ch: 咖啡的苦涩味霸占了你的口腔，这种滋味带着侵略性，向你的味蕾发起残酷的冲锋。
t_en: The bitter flavors of the coffee begin to take over your mouth, aggressively and brutally assaulting your taste buds.

t_ch: 还要浅尝一口。
t_en: Take one more sip.

t_ch: 苦涩的味道似乎被淡化了，味蕾摘下了它的面纱。这杯咖啡背后真实的容颜会是俏丽、温柔、甜美的吗？需要等待……
t_en: The bitterness seems to have dissipated and your taste buds took off the veil that concealed the flavor's true self. Will the real aroma of this coffee turn out to be pretty, gentle, and sweet? One can only wait and see...

t_ch: 再要浅尝一口。
t_en: Go on and take another sip.

t_ch: 果香味逐渐扩散，你好似置身于山谷与鸟鸣之间，而这股涓涓涧溪就是抚慰苦难心灵的最好良药。
t_en: The fruity scent gradually spreads. You seem to be among birdsong in a valley, and the trickling stream before you is the best balm to soothe your suffering soul.

t_ch: 最后浅尝一口。
t_en: Take a final sip.

t_ch: 演出的闭幕往往最为精彩，正如这一口咖啡的余味。
t_en: The concluding act of a show is always the most exciting moment, just like the aftertaste of this cup of coffee.

t_ch: 轻盈的气流将你托起，柔软的羽毛包围着躯干，眼前出现的星光点点也开始左右摇摆……
t_en: A light breeze lifts you up and soft feathers surround your body as the stars before you begin to sway from side to side...

t_ch: 离开这里。
t_en: Leave this place.

t_ch: 终于醒了啊……
t_en: Finally awake, huh?

t_ch: 还以为会出事呢。
t_en: I thought something had gone wrong.

t_ch: 你醒啦？感觉如何？还头晕吗？
t_en: Are you awake? How are you feeling — dizzy?

t_ch: 你一个人在那么危险的地方干嘛？
t_en: What were you doing alone in such a dangerous place?

t_ch: 口腔的苦涩与胃部间歇性的抽搐让你倍感疲乏。你摇摇头，不想回答无关紧要的问题。
t_en: The bitterness in your mouth and intermittent stomach pains leave you feeling extremely weary. You shake your head, not wanting to answer extraneous questions like that.

t_ch: 好吧…你自己小心点，感觉不舒服就去医生那儿看看。
t_en: Alright... take care of yourself, and go see the doctor if you feel sick.

t_ch: 危险的地方少去，下次可不一定这么走运，被咱们救回来啦。
t_en: Stay out of dangerous places. You might not be so lucky to have us save you next time.

t_ch: 面对二人忧心忡忡的神情，你欲言又止。这一定是因为那杯「咖啡」——回忆起它，你不禁忆起舌尖的苦涩和甜美。当初的浅尝辄止是正确的，但，如果再给你一次机会……
t_en: You hold your tongue, seeing the worried look on both of their faces. This must be because of that cup of "coffee." You reminisce on the bitterness and sweetness that was on your tongue. It was the right thing to do to stop after a few sips. However, if you had another chance...

t_ch: 你还会品尝那杯咖啡吗？
t_en: Would you still try that cup of coffee?

t_ch: 还是别喝了。
t_en: I better not drink it.

t_ch: 咖啡固然提神，却也令人失眠。显然，你不想错失任何一场美梦。
t_en: Sure, coffee can add some pep to your step, but it also will make you lose sleep. You clearly don't want to miss a single lovely dream.

t_ch: 算了，不喝了。
t_en: Forget it. I won't drink it.

t_ch: 除了苦涩味，没有别的味道，放下杯子，你开始思索到底什么人会喜欢咖啡。
t_en: It's bitter and doesn't have any other flavor. You put down the cup and start to ponder why anyone could actually like coffee.

t_ch: 杯身里还有不少咖啡，你决定——
t_en: There is still quite a bit of coffee in the cup. You decide to...

t_ch: 咖啡很苦，就像人生一样苦，你决定不再折磨自己。
t_en: Coffee, like life, is utterly bitter. You decide not to torture yourself anymore.

t_ch: 也不知道咖啡在这儿放了多久，考虑到食品安全问题，你果断放下了杯子。
t_en: You have no clue how long this coffee has been sitting here. After thinking about food safety issues, you make firmly put the cup down.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。你敢肯定自己看见了一轮红日坠入雪山，穿着长袍的少女倒在剑戟之下。甚至，你还听见了些微笑声，真真切切，并非幻想……
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. You are sure you saw a red sun descending into the snowy mountains, and a girl in a robe falling under the tip of a sword. You have even heard someone softly laughing. Everything is real, and it is not your imagination...

t_ch: 触碰气泡。
t_en: Touch the bubble.

t_ch: 你伸出手，指尖停留在距气泡极近的地方。什么事也没有发生，也许你应该找了解这东西的人问一问？
t_en: You extend your arm, and your fingertip stops extremely close to the bubble. Nothing happens. Perhaps, you should ask for more info from someone who knows what it is.

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空间站有奇怪的气泡…
t_en: There are strange bubbles at the space station...

t_ch: 啊，这东西可是机密，千万不能让外人知道我有这么个玩意。你得先签个保密协议。
t_en: Oh, this is a top secret. Outsiders can never know that I own this thing. You must sign a non-disclosure agreement first.

t_ch: 里面是什么？
t_en: What's inside?

t_ch: 你没签保密协议前，我一个字也不能说。
t_en: I won't tell you a thing before you sign the agreement.

t_ch: 好，我签。
t_en: Fine. I'll sign it.

t_ch: 很好……
t_en: Great...

t_ch: 我再考虑考虑。
t_en: I'll consider it.

t_ch: 随便你。
t_en: Suit yourself.

t_ch: 你接过黑塔递给你的纸笔，签了自己的名字。纸的质地很奇怪，墨水落在纸上瞬间就干涸了。当笔尖离开纸面时，你的心脏突然悸动。
t_en: You take the paper and pen from Herta and leave your signature. The paper is made from a bizarre material, and the ink dries up the moment it touches the paper. When you move the tip of the pen away, your heart suddenly flutters.

t_ch: 你要知道，这张协议可不仅仅具有法律效应——你必须遵守的是「虚数」的法则，它超乎于一切社会法则与自然法则之上，即使公司也不能打破。
t_en: Please bear in mind that this agreement is more than a legally binding contract. You must also abide by the law of the Imaginary, which is above the social and natural laws. Not even the IPC can override it.

t_ch: 签之前你怎么不说。
t_en: Why didn't you say that before I signed the agreement?

t_ch: 就算说了，难道你会不签吗？你看着就是那种藐视代价的人，先把选择做了，哪管洪水滔天。
t_en: Would you have refused to sign if I'd told you that? You seem like the kind who doesn't think much about consequences. You will make a decision regardless of its price.

t_ch: 「虚数」的法则？
t_en: Law of the Imaginary?

t_ch: 也就是宇宙的法则，世界的法则，唯一仅有牢不可破的法则。
t_en: It is the law of the universe and the world — the only unbreakable law.

t_ch: 能签个废除保密协议的协议吗？
t_en: Can I sign another agreement to nullify this non-disclosure agreement?

t_ch: 放心吧。我真想坑你，你一命呜呼了都不知道怎么回事。
t_en: Don't worry. If I want to swindle you, you'll never see it coming.

t_ch: 行，签了协议，我的忆泡随你玩吧！只要别让流光忆庭的人看见就没问题，其实我觉得看见了也多半没事……
t_en: Now that you have signed the agreement, you can do anything you like with my Memory Bubbles. Just make sure you are out of the sight of entities from the Garden of Recollection. In fact, I don't think there will be any trouble even if they catch you...

t_ch: 忆泡？
t_en: Memory Bubble?

t_ch: 流光忆庭的手段，能够窃取别人的记忆。为了保存记忆，他们用「意质」构建了外膜，把它收藏在像气泡一样的东西里，方便携带。
t_en: The Garden of Recollection has ways to steal the memories of others. To preserve memories, they created a Sentiention membrane to keep the memories wrapped up in it like a bubble, making it easy to carry memories around.

t_ch: 他们还有更高级的技术，叫做「光锥」，那东西捕捉的可不止是记忆了…唉，我没有星神浮黎的祝福，光锥的技术始终搞不定。
t_en: They have an even more advanced technology called Light Cones. With that, they can capture more than just memories... It's a shame that I can never master the Light Cone technology without the Blessing of the Aeon Fuli.

t_ch: 流光忆庭？
t_en: The Garden of Recollection?

t_ch: 崇拜星神「浮黎」的派系，他们觉得认知即世界，记忆则是世界存在的证明。为此，这群人不惜放弃肉身。
t_en: It is a faction that worships the Aeon Fuli. In their opinion, the world is defined by everyone's perception, and memories are the proof of the world's existence. They are willing to forgo their physical bodies for that purpose.

t_ch: 要我说，流光忆庭、筑城者、家族，当然还有反物质军团这群人都是一群傻瓜，崇拜星神崇拜得都魔怔了。
t_en: If you ask me, the Garden of Recollection, the Architects, The Family, and the Antimatter Legion are all idiots. They obsessively worship their Aeons.

t_ch: 忆员是什么？
t_en: Who are the Memokeepers?

t_ch: 流光忆庭的成员，穿行于各世界收集珍贵的记忆。你早晚会遇上他们的，银河列车乘客的记忆，流光忆庭一定特别想要。
t_en: They are members of the Garden of Recollection who travel between worlds to collect precious memories. You will run into them sooner or later. I bet the memory of a Astral Express passenger is highly sought after by them.

t_ch: 碰到文明的忆员未尝不是件好事，他们也许会用神奇的记忆和你交换；但要特别小心流光忆庭里的激进派，那群「窃忆者」——他们偷你记忆时一声招呼都不打。
t_en: You could get lucky if you meet civilized Memokeepers, since they might use an amazing memory to exchange with yours. However, watch out for the Memosnatchers, the radicals in the Garden of Recollection. They won't give you a heads-up before stealing your memory.

t_ch: 现在我能触碰气泡了吗？
t_en: Can I touch the bubble now?

t_ch: 请～用额头接触它即可。记住闭上眼睛，不然会晕。
t_en: Sure~ Just touch it with your forehead. Remember to close your eyes, or your head will start spinning.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：星神。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "Aeon."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 你望着女人在宽檐兜帽遮掩下不大真切的面容，对她方才所述的种种离奇故事感到难以置信。
t_en: Staring at the woman's surreal face concealed under the wide brim of her hood, you can hardly believe the bizarre stories she has just told.

t_ch: 神是存在的？神由凡人擢升而成？神并非全知全能，而是各执权柄、各行其路？这实在是太荒唐了！她竟还自称什么「令使」、什么「揽镜人」……
t_en: Gods really exist, and they are elevated from mortals? Gods aren't almighty, but each holds different authorities and walks different roads? This is too ridiculous! She even claims to be some sort of Emanator and Mirror Holder...

t_ch: 「你感到错乱，」女人微微颔首，「这很正常。」她仿佛能够洞穿你的想法，只以平和的语调冷冷续道。
t_en: The woman nods. "It's normal for you to be confused." She speaks in a calm and cold tone as if she can see through you.

t_ch: 话音将落，她将纤长的手指探入披风，自那绸缎下托出一逸散光彩的锐利物件。你感到眼花缭乱，刚想定睛观瞧，却又被女人开口搅乱了思绪。
t_en: The woman then reaches underneath her silk cloak with her slender fingers and takes out a gleaming, sharp object. Dazzled by the glow, you try to take a close look, only to be distracted again by the woman's voice.

t_ch: 「这是『超验之镜』，」她的音色依旧冷清，「看看祂吧——看看『星神』的样子。」
t_en: "This is the Mirror of Transcendence. Look at THEM. Look at what the Aeon look like." The woman's voice remains frosty.

t_ch: 好奇心迫使你向其中窥伺。你未能如预料般从镜中看到自己，反而是一尊华美的雕塑。那雕塑自上而下裂解，通体如断桩遭雷击，向两侧剥离，各自延展。
t_en: You stare into the mirror as curiosity gets the better of you. Instead of your reflection, you see a magnificent sculpture. The sculpture shatters from top to bottom as if it is struck by lightning. It splits vertically into halves, and the separated parts fall independently.

t_ch: 属于雕塑左侧的似是一健美的男性，而右侧则像是绮丽的女子；每侧各有双臂，其掌攀向对侧的躯体，相互支撑、托举，又执着刻刀，仿佛正在雕琢其本身。
t_en: The left part of the sculpture is a muscular man, while the right part is a gorgeous woman. Each part has two arms extended toward the other. They support and uplift each other, but also hold a carving knife each as though they are crafting each other.

t_ch: 两对身躯虬曲着，纠缠着，舞动着，俨然一道石质的螺旋；却又自腰部以下融合，共享同一半身。那姿态十足诡丽，令你如痴如醉，心荡神驰。
t_en: The pair of twisting, entangled, and intertwined bodies look like a spiral made of stone. Yet, they are joined together from their waists down. You are mesmerized and fascinated by the remarkably gorgeous and bizarre posture.

t_ch: 恍惚间，你竟发觉镜中之人的双眸在某一刹那与你的视线相接——你震栗，即刻从超然的体验中剥离。「这就是你们的神…」你喃喃自语。
t_en: Suddenly, your gaze meets the eyes of the being in the mirror. Stunned, you quickly withdraw yourself from the extraordinary experience. "So, this is your god..." you mumble.

t_ch: 「不，这只是你认为最『纯美』的事物。」女人摇头，摩挲手中的碎镜：「而这正是祂*本身*。」
t_en: "No. This is what beauty in its purest form looks like in your perception." The woman shakes her head as she strokes the cracked mirror in her hands. "This is what THEY ARE."

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：瑰丽的星球。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "a splendid planet."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 「嘿，卡罗，」你身旁的男性望着舷窗外，那颗悬于无垠深空中的碧玉珠翠，「这是第几次星出了？」
t_en: "Hey, Caro." The man next to you stares out the porthole at a sparkling jade-like object hovering in the boundless space. "How many times has the star rise happened?"

t_ch: 「呃…我看看，」你用小小的手掌握着太空笔，笔尖从表格的顶端自上而下，「<unbreak>27</unbreak>次？」你咕哝着。「嗯，<unbreak>27</unbreak>次。」你舔舔嘴唇。其实你还不太能认字。
t_en: "Um... Let me see..." Holding a space pen in your small palm, you trace at the form from top to bottom with the tip of the pen. "<unbreak>27</unbreak> times, I guess?" You mutter. "Yes, <unbreak>27</unbreak> times." You lick your lips. Actually, you are not quite literate yet.

t_ch: 「又一个系统月过去了。」他叹气，「你说，我们什么时候才能回去？」
t_en: "Another system month has passed." He sighs. "When do you think we can go back?"

t_ch: 「地面上已经没有人能引导我们着陆了——我们甚至进入不了大气层！」你摸了摸胡须：「如果他们也能从这个视角看看她…悲剧或许就不会发生了。」
t_en: "There's no one left on the planetary surface to guide our landing. We can't even make it to the atmosphere!" You touch your whiskers. "If only they could look at the planet from this angle... Then the tragedy might not have happened."

t_ch: 「你看看她…这就是我们曾经的家。这就是曾经的我们。在这里曾经生活着每一个人——你爱的人，你认识的人——每一个人！无论是谁，都将在此度过一生……
t_en: "Look at her... This used to be our home and our past. Everyone, including the people you love and know, stayed there! People were originally bound to spend their entire lives there regardless of who they are..."

t_ch: 「我们的所有喜怒哀乐，我们的所有宗教信仰，我们的所有意识形态…所有的猎人与强盗、国王与农民、英雄与懦夫，所有的父母、还有他们满怀希望的孩子……
t_en: "All of our emotions, religions, and ideologies... All the hunters and robbers, kings and peasants, heroes and cowards, parents and their children..."

t_ch: 「我们的一切所见、所闻、所思、所想…一切的一切，全都存在于这个*黯点*上——一粒悬浮在射线暴与恒星光中的微尘，一个发着绿色微光的黯点！
t_en: "Everything we see, hear, think, and feel exists on this DARK SPOT — a speck of dust floating in ray bursts and the sparkle of stars, a dark spot that radiates a feeble green glow!"

t_ch: 「你看看她，」你扬起手，「那帮老掉牙的政客…竟然以为这个黯点就是世界的中心，他们就因此在宇宙中享有生杀大权！还有比这更可笑的事吗？」
t_en: "Look at her." You lift your hand. "How dare those ancient politicians assume this dark spot to be the center of the world, and therefore meant they have the power to control the universe? Could there be anything more ridiculous than that?"

t_ch: 「哇哦。」他看着你，表情显得有些不可思议：「真没想到…你还是个演讲家。」
t_en: "Wow." He stares at you with an incredulous expression. "I didn't know you were so articulate."

t_ch: 「得了吧，你也知道我识不得几个字，」你甩了甩尾巴，「我可是只*老鼠*啊！」
t_en: "Come on, you know I can't even read well." You flick your tail. "I'm just a RAT!"

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 平平无奇的小机器人，为空间站提供着平平无奇的生活服务。
t_en: A very ordinary little robot providing very ordinary life services aboard the space station.

t_ch: 你注意到机器人身上有一个凸出的按钮，看上去像是电源标志，你决定——
t_en: You notice a button sticking out of the robot, looking like it might be a power button. You decide to —

t_ch: 帮它强制关机！
t_en: Force a shutdown!

t_ch: 你注意到小机器人在此待机已久，你按下电源键，小机器人进入了休眠模式。
t_en: You notice that the little robot has been on standby here for some time. You push the power button, sending the little robot into sleep mode.

t_ch: 你为空间站节省了些许能源，尽管不多，但某种满足感令你不由自主地挺起了胸膛！
t_en: You've saved just a teensy bit of power for the space station. It's not much, but it's still enough to make you puff your chest out in pride!

t_ch: 还是别碰了。
t_en: Don't touch it.

t_ch: 小机器人继续在此待机，等待空间站的工作调配。
t_en: The little robot continues humming along in standby mode, waiting for its next task to be allocated.

t_ch: 房间里矗立着一个平平无奇的…家用设备。瞧着它格格不入的模样，你怀疑自己是否走错了地方。
t_en: Standing upright in the middle of the room is an unremarkable... Err, household "object." It looks so out of place that you start to suspect you might have taken a wrong turn somewhere.

t_ch: 陶瓷质地的表面上贴着张便条：「奇物『新世界大门』已被应物科████实验借用，非实验人员眼看手勿动。」
t_en: There's a note stuck to the ceramic surface: "Curio: Door to A New World, loaned to ████ at the Department of Implement Arts for experiments. Non-research personnel should not touch this object."

t_ch: 在某个按钮附近还贴着一张警告：「未经许可坐下以及按下冲水按钮，造成的一切后果本科室概不承担。」
t_en: There's also a warning posted near a particular button: "The Department is not responsible for any consequences from unauthorized persons sitting down and pressing flush."

t_ch: 警告的内容与其说是警示，倒不如说是某种使用教程。你决定——
t_en: It's not so much a warning but a tutorial. You decide to —

t_ch: 坐上去，按冲水键！
t_en: Sit down and flush!

t_ch: 你坐上「新世界大门」，刚按下冲水键，一股古怪的吸力传来——
t_en: You sit down on the Door to A New World. As soon as you push the flush button, you start to feel a strange suction —

t_ch: 「新世界大门」将你带到了危险地带，想要离开可并不容易。
t_en: The Door to A New World has brought you to a dangerous place, and it isn't going to be easy to get out of here.

t_ch: 怪物身手不凡，但还好你技高一筹，有惊无险。
t_en: The monster has extraordinary skills, but you're much stronger still. The affair was more of a shock than any real danger.

t_ch: 还是赶紧按下冲水键离开这里吧。
t_en: Just flush it and get outta here.

t_ch: 你这人怎么回事，吓我一跳！
t_en: Who the — what the!? You scared the heck out of me!

t_ch: 呃呃我有点晕…
t_en: Uhhh... I'm kinda dizzy...

t_ch: 啊，抱歉，你是生病了吗，还是去医疗科看看吧。
t_en: Oh no, are you sick? You should go to the Medical Department and get that checked out.

t_ch: 你看到一个马桶没有？
t_en: Have you seen a toilet bowl around here?

t_ch: 什么马桶？这里又不是厕所…等等！这是什么？！
t_en: Toilet bowl? What toilet bowl? This isn't a restroom... Hang on! What's this!?

t_ch: 我就偷个懒，怎么会不断有人冒出来？还有这个…马桶？！又是这个玩意！
t_en: I just want to slack off for a while, but why are all these people popping out of nowhere? And this... toilet!? Not this again!

t_ch: 吓到你了？抱歉抱歉。
t_en: Did I scare you? Sorry.

t_ch: 算了，不至于不至于。
t_en: Nah, it's not that bad.

t_ch: 「不断有人冒出来」？
t_en: "Popping out of nowhere"?

t_ch: 呃是的…就跟你刚刚一样。
t_en: Uh, yeah... Like you just did right now.

t_ch: 怎么说呢，这个地方本来是我的「专属休息区」，有时候实验数据不理想，我就过来休息一下。
t_en: How to put it... This place was once my own private rest area. Whenever experiments wouldn't go my way, I'd come here for a little break.

t_ch: 但最近总遇到奇怪的事，比如说我在走神的时候，偶尔会听到惊呼声——哦对了，每次都有冲水声！
t_en: But recently, I keep on running into weird things. Like, when I was daydreaming, I'd sometimes hear startled cries — oh yeah, and always with this sound of flushing water!

t_ch: 我就纳闷了，我又不是在厕所休息，怎么会听到冲水声！而且这次还真冒出来个「马桶」！到底什么情况！
t_en: I got so confused — I wasn't in the restroom, so how come there's this flushing sound!? And then this "toilet bowl" just popped out of nowhere!

t_ch: （告知你所了解的情况）
t_en: (Recount what you know)

t_ch: 哦…原来如此…我明白了，谢谢。
t_en: Oh... So that's how it is then... I got it. Thank you.

t_ch: 那个…这里是我的「专属休息区」，没什么事的话能不能请你暂时先出去，我想清净会儿……
t_en: Um... This is my private rest area. If you don't mind, could I please ask you to leave for a bit? I'd like a little peace and quiet...

t_ch: 一阵令人不适的眩晕感后，你被「新世界大门」带到了主控舱段。
t_en: After a period of uncomfortable dizziness, you are transported to the master control zone by the Door to A New World.

t_ch: 迎接你的并不是一场波澜壮阔的冒险，但就这样平平淡淡才是真也没什么不好。
t_en: No magnificent adventure greets you, but you're okay with this level of mundanity.

t_ch: 一阵令人不适的眩晕感后，你被「新世界大门」带到了基座舱段。
t_en: After a period of uncomfortable dizziness, you are transported to the base zone by the Door to A New World.

t_ch: 一阵令人不适的眩晕感后，你被「新世界大门」带到了收容舱段。
t_en: After a period of uncomfortable dizziness, you are transported to the storage zone by the Door to A New World.

t_ch: 一个平平无奇的…家用设备。
t_en: It is a common... household item.

t_ch: 在某个按钮附近贴着一张警告：「未经许可坐下以及按下冲水按钮，造成的一切后果本科室概不承担。」
t_en: There's a button with a warning posted beside it: "Our department is not responsible for any consequences suffered by those who sit down and press Flush without permission."

t_ch: 你决定——
t_en: You decide to...

t_ch: 坐上去，按冲水键！
t_en: Sit down and flush!

t_ch: 你坐上「新世界大门」，刚按下冲水键，一股古怪的吸力传来——
t_en: You sit down on the Door to A New World. As soon as you push the button, you start to feel a strange suctioning force —

t_ch: 一阵令人不适的眩晕感后，你被「新世界大门」带到了空间站的某处。
t_en: After a period of uncomfortable dizziness, you are transported somewhere at the space station by the Door to A New World.

t_ch: 冠军！请给我签名！
t_en: Champion! Could I pleeeease get your autograph?

t_ch: 他的肌肉…我要晕了！
t_en: His muscles... I'm going to faint!

t_ch: 卢卡哥哥！卢卡哥哥！
t_en: Luka! Luka! Luka! Lukaaaa!

t_ch: 不要挤，要守秩序。
t_en: Don't crowd around, have some order.

t_ch: 人人有份，都别着急。
t_en: Everyone will have their share, don't worry.

t_ch: 机械臂可不能摸哦！
t_en: Don't touch the robot arm!

t_ch: 不愧是冠军，这人气！
t_en: How popular! As expected of the champ.

t_ch: 真忙啊，卢卡小哥！
t_en: Busy times, huh, Luka!

t_ch: 唷，这不是搏击俱乐部冉冉升起的新星{NICKNAME}吗！
t_en: Whew, if it isn't the Fight Club rising star, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 我听说你们一伙人早早就飞出地表，回太空里行侠仗义去了——这不还没走嘛？
t_en: I thought you lot already left this planet for more chivalrous adventures in space, but you're still here!

t_ch: 怎么，手痒了，特地跑来想过把拳击瘾？这可不好办啊，现在是休赛期……
t_en: Did you come all the way here to scratch your boxing itch? This might be a bit tough, since it's the off-season now...

t_ch: 那个红头发的是谁？
t_en: Who's that redhead?

t_ch: 什么？！你在俱乐部混了这么久了，居然没听说过那位？
t_en: What!? You've been in the club for so long, but you still haven't heard of him?

t_ch: 告诉你吧，那位可是连续二十七届搏击擂主挑战赛的冠军，「熔锤镇的卢卡」！
t_en: Let me tell you, he's the <unbreak>27</unbreak>-time champion of the Fighting King Challenge, "Luka of Moltammerville"!

t_ch: 你这儿挺热闹啊。
t_en: You've got quite a crowd here.

t_ch: 那是当然，也不看看是谁回来了！
t_en: Of course. Just look who's back!

t_ch: 旁边那位——对，就是正被粉丝团团围住的那个红头发少年——可是连续二十七届搏击擂主挑战赛的冠军，「熔锤镇的卢卡」！
t_en: That guy - yes, that red-haired boy surrounded by fans - is the <unbreak>27</unbreak>-time champion of the Fighting King Challenge, "Luka of Moltammerville"!

t_ch: 卢卡小哥可是奥列格首领的得意门生，还是「地火」最靠得住的干部之一！即便如此，他还是以性格平易近人著称——这可不是件容易事！
t_en: Luka is the favorite student of Chief Oleg, and one of the most reliable members of Wildfire! He's also known for being approachable despite it all. That's not an easy ask!

t_ch: 我也是冠军，怎么没这待遇？
t_en: I, too, am a champion. Where's my crowd of adoring fans?

t_ch: 呃…我不都说了吗，人家是连续蝉联二十七届的超级冠军！
t_en: Um... I did say Luka is a <unbreak>27</unbreak>-time super champion, no?

t_ch: 粉丝不都是靠成绩积累赢来的吗？别急，我打赌，你很快也能拥有自己的后援会了！
t_en: You get more fans by accumulating achievements! Don't worry, I bet you'll have your own fan club soon!

t_ch: 他之前干嘛去了？
t_en: Why did he leave?

t_ch: 呃，我上哪知道去？我猜无非是去执行「地火」的任务，帮下层区别的镇子抵御裂界之类的吧？
t_en: My guess is as good as yours. I figure he probably went on some Wildfire mission to help the other Underworld towns fight against the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 别看现在馆里这么热闹，但其实那帮粉丝都是三分钟热度…我最了解这群人了。你要是想跟卢卡小哥聊聊，就等他们散了再过去吧？
t_en: The club might be crowded now, but these fans won't be sticking around for long... I've seen this all before. If you want to chat with Luka, just wait till the fans leave.

t_ch: 卢卡，你最近在研究什么新的必杀技吗？
t_en: Luka, have you been working on some new ultimate technique lately?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，这可是秘密！还是赛场上见真章吧！
t_en: Hahaha, well, that's a secret! You'll have to wait and see.

t_ch: 卢卡哥哥，你以后会教我们拳击技术吗？
t_en: Big Brother Luka, will you teach us how to fight in the future?

t_ch: 有机会的话，一定！前提是你们得好好学习，好好吃饭！
t_en: If the chance arises, then definitely! On the condition that you work hard and eat well!

t_ch: （七嘴八舌的…感觉插不上话啊。）
t_en: (There're so many people talking... I don't think I can get a word in.)

t_ch: （还是等人群散了再回来吧。）
t_en: (I'll come back after the crowd has died down.)

t_ch: …嗯？不好意思，我刚才在思考些事情！你是来要签名的吗？还是……
t_en: ...Hmm? Sorry, I was just thinking about something! Are you here for an autograph? Or...

t_ch: 等等，这身装扮…绝对没错，你就是那位大名鼎鼎的「开拓者」！
t_en: Wait, your outfit... There's no mistake, you must be the famous "Trailblazer"!

t_ch: 幸会，我是{NICKNAME}。
t_en: What an honor to meet ya, I'm {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 哪里哪里，该感到荣幸的人是我才对！你的名字，奥列格师父跟希儿他们都经常提及。
t_en: No, no, the honor's all mine! Master Oleg and Seele have mentioned your name many times before.

t_ch: 没错，我就是贝洛伯格的传奇。
t_en: You're right. I'm the legend of Belobog.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，跟奥列格师父和希儿他们说的一样，你的确是个有趣的家伙！
t_en: Hahaha, you're just as Master Oleg and Seele described. You really are an interesting one!

t_ch: 可惜！我前段时间一直在支援磐岩镇周边的镇子，起早贪黑地跟裂界里冒出来的妖魔鬼怪打交道，完全错过了你们拯救世界的大冒险……
t_en: What a pity! I was supporting the folks around Boulder Town, dealing with the monsters emerging from the Fragmentum from dawn to dusk. I completely missed you lot's big saving-the-world adventure...

t_ch: 不过能在镇上偶遇你这位大英雄，也算是弥补了些遗憾了！
t_en: But being able to meet a great hero such as yourself right now more than makes up for it!

t_ch: 哦，太激动一下忘了，我还没正式自我介绍——我叫卢卡，跟希儿一样是「地火」的干部。奥列格首领你应该很熟了吧？他是教我战斗技巧的师父。
t_en: Oh, I got so excited that I almost forgot to formally introduce myself. My name is Luka, and I'm a core member of "Wildfire" just like Seele. You should be very familiar with Chief Oleg, right? He is the master who taught me how to fight.

t_ch: 怎么不提你<unbreak>27</unbreak>届冠军的事？
t_en: Why didn't you mention your <unbreak>27</unbreak> championship victories?

t_ch: 嗯？哈哈哈，原来这你都知道了啊！
t_en: Huh? Hahaha, so you've heard about that, too!

t_ch: 我觉得这算不上什么，跟你们为贝洛伯格做的事比起来，实在不值一提！
t_en: I didn't think it was anything worth mentioning compared to what you people have done for Belobog!

t_ch: 我不是为了争夺什么荣誉才参加搏击比赛的…嗐，听着是不是有点故作谦虚的意思？但我真的是这么想的，你得信我！
t_en: I didn't compete for glory or anything... Now that I listen to myself say that, it does sound rather pretentious, doesn't it? But it's true. Please believe me!

t_ch: 你为史考特打拳吗？
t_en: Do you fight for Scott?

t_ch: 史考特？
t_en: Scott?

t_ch: 哦…你说的是俱乐部的老板史考特先生吧？没有，我跟他不熟。
t_en: Umm... You must be referring to the Fight Club's owner, Mr. Scott? No, I'm not acquainted with him.

t_ch: 老实说，我不太喜欢他搞的那套规矩，拳手经纪人什么的…我觉得没必要，太浮夸了。我只想靠自己的实力说话，其它的一概不感兴趣。
t_en: To be honest, I don't really like the set of rules he made. Boxing managers and all that... I don't really think it's necessary. It's too pretentious. I just want to let my own abilities do the talking, and I am not interested in anything else.

t_ch: 最近「地火」在忙什么呢？
t_en: What has Wildfire been up to recently?

t_ch: 多亏了你们，我们最近的活计都轻松了不少。
t_en: Thanks to you lot, everything's been going much more smoothly for us lately.

t_ch: 日常的放哨巡逻当然不能落下，偶尔还要帮街里街坊的解决些小矛盾…哦，对了，最近还要配合铁卫搞几场联合演练，这事不能忘。
t_en: Of course, the daily sentry and patrol duties can't be dropped, and occasionally I have to help to resolve some small conflicts in the neighborhood... Oh, I shouldn't forget to work with the Silvermane Guards to conduct a few joint exercises soon.

t_ch: 以后有机会一起切磋。
t_en: Let's spar together if we have the chance.

t_ch: 没问题！能跟拯救了贝洛伯格的「开拓者」一较高下，这绝对是千载难逢的机会！
t_en: Of course! Being able to trade notes with the savior of Belobog would be a rare opportunity!

t_ch: 看你这架势，是要赶着去帮助其他遇到困难的人吧？哈哈哈，我从老远就能嗅到乐于助人的味道！
t_en: You look like you're rushing off to help others in trouble, right? Hahaha, I can smell the willingness to help from miles away!

t_ch: 回见，{NICKNAME}！希望还有机会在下层区遇到你。
t_en: See you later, {NICKNAME}! I hope I'll have a chance to see you in the Underworld again!

t_ch: 唷，{NICKNAME}！在附近闲逛吗？
t_en: Yo, {NICKNAME}! Were you just in the neighborhood?

t_ch: 我要先歇一会儿再去做下一组锻炼，正好可以陪你聊两句！
t_en: I was just resting for a bit before getting to my next set of exercises, and I'm just in time to chat with you!

t_ch: 听说你是<unbreak>27</unbreak>届搏击冠军？
t_en: So I hear you're the <unbreak>27</unbreak>-time champion?

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：家族。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "The Family."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 你从未想过自己还能坐在这里。富丽堂皇的剧院，豪华包厢，丝滑的红色天鹅绒沙发里填充着轻盈的皮埃尔鸭绒…除了「上流」，你想象不到任何形容词了。
t_en: You have never imagined yourself to be sitting in the lavish box of a grand theater. The silky red velvet sofa is stuffed with Pierduck down feathers. You can't find a better word to describe the sofa other than "extravagant."

t_ch: 舞台正上演着一出你不知内容的古典歌剧。《国王寻欢作乐》——如果你没记错的话，这应该是那剧目高贵的名字。其实，你可以问问身旁的雷诺阿先生……
t_en: An unfamiliar classical opera is playing on the stage. "The King's Pleasure" ...If you are not mistaken, that's the opera's elegant title. Actually, you could ask Mr. Renoir, who's sitting next to you, about it...

t_ch: 思来想去，你还是放弃了求证。反正也看不懂，你想，我只是奉命来调查「家族」的…如果一切顺利，或许能够拿到通向匹诺康尼的邀请函——这才是你的目的。
t_en: After some consideration, you decide not to ask anything, since you cannot understand what's going on anyway. After all, you're only here to investigate The Family... If everything goes well, you might get your hands on an invitation letter to Penacony — that's what you are after.

t_ch: 你不想节外生枝，便又装作聚精会神似的盯着舞台上的演员，看他们纵情宣叙，引吭高歌。你又以余光打量着雷诺阿……
t_en: Not wanting any trouble, you pretend to be engrossed in the performance and songs of the actors onstage. At the same time, you size Renoir up from the corner of your eye...

t_ch: 他似乎注意到了你。「先生，」他以那惯常一丝不苟的微笑示人，「这剧目令您感到厌烦了么？」你无法从他的话语中察觉任何情绪…这令你有些许不适。
t_en: Renoir seems to notice you. "Sir," he asks with his usual impeccable smile, "do you find the opera boring?" The fact that you cannot detect any emotions in his words makes you feel uncomfortable.

t_ch: 「不不不，」你连忙摆手，「我只是…有些好奇。」雷诺阿先生不发一语地注视着你，仿佛在示意你继续——他最好是，你想——不然这趟可白来了……
t_en: "Not at all." You quickly wave your hand dismissively. "I'm just... curious." Mr. Renoir watches you silently, as if asking you to continue — well, he better be — otherwise you would've wasted your time...

t_ch: 「那『家族』…果真存在么？」你试探。他摆头，抬手示意侍应生上前。那位年轻的侍应手举托盘，将其上所呈之物向你二人展示。
t_en: "Does The Family... really exist?" You ask tentatively. Renoir shakes his head and beckons a waiter over. The young waiter presents an item on a tray to the two of you.

t_ch: 一只精美木匣，侧面开一狭长小口。你正好奇这是什么时，却听得雷诺阿含混不清道：「放进去。」——不…那真的是他的声音吗？你确信自己听到了*杂声*……
t_en: It is an exquisite wooden box with a long and narrow opening on its side. As you wonder what it is, you hear Renoir speaking in a muddled voice. "Put it in." No... Is that Renoir's voice? You are sure you heard a NOISE...

t_ch: 尽管那指令极不清晰，你还是凭本能将手掌置入其中。但为什么？你为这下意识的反应感到震悚…随后，你感到一股莫之能御的愧疚感席卷全身——
t_en: Although the instruction is unclear, you still place your hand in the box out of instinct. But why? You are shocked by your reaction... Next, you experience an overwhelming sense of guilt surging through your body...

t_ch: 就像是一名听候宣判的罪犯。是的…你确信，在那一瞬间，你感受到了目光——尖冷，刺骨…来自雷诺阿先生，来自侍应生，来自整个剧院。
t_en: You feel like a criminal waiting for judgment. Yes... You are certain that looks are being leveled at you — penetrating, icy gazes from Mr. Renoir, the waiter, and everyone else in the theater.

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：背叛。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "Betrayal."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 你以直径<unbreak>20</unbreak>厘米的<unbreak>160</unbreak>度广角镜头环视会场。<unbreak>46</unbreak>根大理石石柱将直径<unbreak>43</unbreak>米、高<unbreak>138</unbreak>米的穹顶高擎，使这东西宽<unbreak>190</unbreak>米、南北长<unbreak>310</unbreak>米的柱廊大厅显得十足宽阔。
t_en: You scan the venue through a <unbreak>160</unbreak>-degree wide-angle lens with a diameter of <unbreak>20</unbreak> cm. Thanks to the <unbreak>46</unbreak> marble pillars that support the dome ceiling with a diameter of <unbreak>43</unbreak> meters and a height of <unbreak>138</unbreak> meters, the colonnaded hall <unbreak>190</unbreak> meters wide and <unbreak>310</unbreak> meters long appears spacious.

t_ch: 与会人数应有<unbreak>138</unbreak>人，实际到场<unbreak>136</unbreak>人。其中<unbreak>42</unbreak>人位于你左侧，<unbreak>44</unbreak>人位于右侧，<unbreak>49</unbreak>人与你遥遥相对，而最后1人正在你身下不远处的讲台上慷慨陈词。
t_en: The actual number of attendees is <unbreak>136</unbreak> when it should be <unbreak>138</unbreak>. <unbreak>42</unbreak> of them are on your left side, <unbreak>44</unbreak> are on your right, <unbreak>49</unbreak> are on your opposite end in the distance, while the last one is giving a speech on the stage below not far away from you.

t_ch: 「女士们，先生们，」此人语音声压级约为<unbreak>45</unbreak>分贝，「现在，在各位眼前所展示的正是利尔他-雅各宾数理院的最新成果——普朗托专家系统！」
t_en: "Ladies and gentlemen!" This person is speaking at a volume of about <unbreak>45</unbreak> decibels. "Now, we proudly present the latest product of the Aiden-Jacobin School of Mathematics and Science, the Pronto Expert System!"

t_ch: 席下爆发出热烈的掌声。那人续道：「普朗托是我国首部拥有强人工智能的超级电脑，搭载了自然语言及图形处理、情感行为识别反馈等划时代的功能……」
t_en: A thunderous applause erupts from the audience. The speaker continues. "Pronto is the first supercomputer with powerful artificial intelligence in our country. It features groundbreaking functions, such as natural language and graphics processing, and emotional and behavioral recognition and responses..."

t_ch: 语音声压级提升至<unbreak>61</unbreak>分贝了。这令你的压力感应膜感到*不适*。但你被教导应服从人类命令，因此你并没有生成音频信号，向人群传达你的*主观感受*。
t_en: The speaker's volume increases to <unbreak>61</unbreak> decibels, causing DISCOMFORT to your pressure-sensing membrane. As you have been trained to obey human instructions, you do not generate audio signals to express your SUBJECTIVE FEELINGS to the crowd.

t_ch: 「…而在本次纺锤-Ⅴ的探索任务中，普朗托将革命性地成为科考船的『领航员』，以第六名乘员的身份……」那人神采飞扬。
t_en: "...and in the coming Spindle-V mission, Pronto will unprecedentedly pilot the research vessel as the sixth passenger..." The speaker is in high spirits.

t_ch: 一簇紊乱的智能脉冲掠过你硅芯片基板<unbreak>42</unbreak>区，又在<unbreak>56</unbreak>区消失无踪。你很清楚，自己已经对这类陈腔滥调感到*厌烦*了——那道不稳定的脉冲就是证明。
t_en: A cluster of disordered intelligent pulses passes through Area <unbreak>42</unbreak> of your silicon chip substrate and vanishes in Area <unbreak>56</unbreak>. You know you are SICK OF the cliche — the unstable pulse is proof.

t_ch: 这时席间有人起身。「普朗托，」那人的语气中带有试探，又像是在质问，「你在此次行动中责任重大——海量的任务是否会令你感觉有失信心？」
t_en: At that moment, someone from the audience stands up. "Pronto," the person speaks in a tentative and questioning tone, "you are assigned a very important role in the mission. Will your confidence be swayed by the amount of work?"

t_ch: 你*轻蔑*地增大<unbreak>78</unbreak>区的电压，令电磁阀驱动快门喀地一响。随后，你调度电机，将机械臂扭向正确的角度：「让我这么回答你吧，先生。」你的振膜吱呀作响。
t_en: DISDAINFULLY, you increase the voltage in Area <unbreak>78</unbreak>, moving the shutter of the solenoid valve with a click. Then, you adjust the motor to move your mechanical arm to the correct angle. "Let me answer you this way, sir." Your diaphragm creaks.

t_ch: 「普朗托专家系统是有史以来最可靠的人工智能，从未出现过任何错误或信息失真，」你开始发声，「我绝对*名副其实*，*万无一失*，且*毫无差错*。」
t_en: "The Pronto Expert System is the most reliable artificial intelligence in history. I've never had errors or produced distorted information," you say. "I am GENUINELY IMPECCABLE and FLAWLESS."

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：纯粹的信仰。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "Pure faith."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 你端坐在华美的祭坛中央。你见到许多人身披缀着金丝的白袍，而另一些人顶盔掼甲，肃穆地围立四周，喃喃低语，不带感情的嗡鸣充斥整个空旷的岩窟。
t_en: You sit upright in the center of a splendid altar. You see many people wearing white robes embroidered with gold threads, while others are standing solemnly around the altar in armor and helmets. Their monotonous murmurs fill up the empty rocky cavern.

t_ch: 你很清楚他们在做什么：他们在呼唤祂，呼唤那位逆时而行的尊贵者…他们意图通过隐写某个神秘的通讯频道，将其编纂成仪式祷文——
t_en: It is apparent to you what they are doing — they are summoning THEM, the noble being who travels against time... They intend to treat a mysterious communication channel with steganography and craft it into a ritual prayer...

t_ch: 通过灵性的典仪，他们编织时间的丝缕，意图自创世之树凋落的最后一片叶向起始的萌芽溯行。他们坚信如此便能与尊贵之人相遇，尽管那瞬间稍纵即逝。
t_en: Through a spiritual ritual, they weave the threads of time in an attempt to trace backwards from the last withered leaf on the World Tree to the first bud in the beginning of everything. They firmly believe that by doing so, they will get to meet the noble being, though the encounter will be brief and fleeting.

t_ch: 你很好奇他们中是否有人曾达成了目标——呃，你绝无亵渎他们信念的意思——毕竟你坚信时间是单向的，绝无回环的余地。如此想来，他们的教义就很扯淡……
t_en: You wonder whether some of them have achieved the goal. Without the intention of disrespecting their faith, you strongly opine that time is unidirectional, and it is impossible to go back in it. When you take that into account, their creed sounds ridiculous...

t_ch: 这时，离你最近的那位白袍猛然抬头。难道他们能感知你的*思想*？你开始恐慌：进一步地，他们会如何处置你？话说回来，他们从一开始就请你登上了祭坛——
t_en: At that moment, the one in the white robe closest to you suddenly looks up. Can they sense your THOUGHTS? Anxiety seizes you as you wonder what they will do to you. But then again, it was them who invited you to the altar in the first place —

t_ch: 难道…这是某种残忍的献祭仪式吗？！你开始颤抖，并大声央求他们饶你一命。可惜的是，你没有嘴——好吧，这下你只能寄希望于他们确实能偷窥思想了……
t_en: Could this be... a cruel ritual that involves sacrifices!? You tremble in fear and plead for their mercy loudly, but you do not have a mouth. Now, you really wish they could read your mind...

t_ch: 那白袍上前，向你徐徐走来。天啊，你哀嚎，至少用些人道的方式吧！你几乎能看到那人袍子下闪烁的寒光了…救命，这地方还有没有王法了？！你紧闭双眼。
t_en: The person in the white robe walks up to you at a steady pace. "Oh, please at least treat me humanely!" You wail. You can almost make out the cold glint flickering underneath the person's robe... Help! Are there no law and order here!? You keep your eyes tightly shut.

t_ch: 你听见那可憎的长袍窸窸窣窣地在石质地面上拖行，以及那对赤足坠在地面上的闷响。声音越来越近、越来越大——然后在距你咫尺之远的位置戛然而止。
t_en: You hear the rustling sound of that annoying robe being dragged along the stone tiles and the muffled thump of a pair of bare feet landing on the ground. The sound gets closer, louder, and suddenly comes to an abrupt halt somewhere very close to you.

t_ch: 然而，你只感觉到一丝温暖的触感。你睁眼，发觉那白袍只是将额头抵在你身上。你见那兜帽的阴影中流下两行热泪：「纪念碑在颤抖…祂感知到我们了！」
t_en: Yet, you only feel a warm sensation. You open your eyes to find the person pressing his forehead against your body. Tears are streaming down his face, which was obscured by the shadow of the hood. "The monument is shaking... THEY have sensed us!"

t_ch: 他大声宣告。随后，解脱的喟叹自人群中炸裂开来。你也感到一阵解脱——太好了，看来大家都是文明人——没人会对一块可怜的*石头*做些什么的……
t_en: He announces at the top of his lungs. Soon after, sighs of relief come from the crowd. A surge of relief washes over you, too. It's great that people here are civilized, and no one will do anything bad to a pathetic STONE...

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：握紧的双手。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "Tightly-held hands."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 你的神经元与<unbreak>16</unbreak>号机甲的手臂骨骼连接在一起，这种感觉从来都不舒服。你扭过头想对搭档抱怨，然后，你发现莉亚也在看你，话语不再有必要了。
t_en: Your neurons are connected to the bones of Mecha No. <unbreak>16</unbreak>'s arms — the experience has always been uncomfortable. You turn around, ready to complain to your partner, only to find Leah is also staring at you. No words are necessary.

t_ch: 她笑，然后扬手。你感到小臂有了反应，像提线木偶，被看不见的线牵扯。你想对抗，但发觉有些疼，于是你也扬手。痛感果然减轻了。这在你预料之内。
t_en: She smiles, then raises her arm. You sense a reaction in your forearm. Like a marionette, you are controlled by invisible strings. You try to resist it, but it hurts. At last, you lift your arm, and the pain lessens as expected.

t_ch: 「不喜欢像这样被我控制？」她歪头，对你咯咯笑着，眨眨眼，双手又立食指与中指，举过头顶，好像一对兔耳。不得已，你也如此照做。你觉得实在羞耻。
t_en: "You don't like being controlled by me in this way?" She snickers at you with her head crooked. Blinking her eyes, she sticks up her index and middle fingers and rests both hands on her head, making them look like a pair of rabbit ears. You are forced to do the same and feel embarrassed about it.

t_ch: 你知道她在故意戏弄你：「能别趁机读取我的想法吗？」你故意皱眉，又很快转过头去，避免与她的目光交汇。你从来不适应这个。
t_en: You know she is making fun of you. "Could you not read my mind?" You deliberately crease your forehead and quickly spin around to avoid meeting her eyes. This is not something you are used to doing.

t_ch: 莉亚轻轻哼笑，又将脸故意凑上来。「<unbreak>16</unbreak>号根本没这个功能，」你感觉到温润的气息扑打着你后颈的皮肤，「你在害羞吗？明明也是女孩子……」
t_en: Leah chuckles before inching her face closer. "No. <unbreak>16</unbreak> isn't capable of that." You can feel her warm breath caressing the back of your neck. "Are you blushing? C'mon, you are a girl too."

t_ch: 你决定结束这个令人尴尬的话题。「我们这次的任务是什么？」你咬牙，喉头含着热炭似的，浑身燥热难耐，发出的声音也变得支离顿挫。
t_en: You decide to steer the conversation away from the awkward topic. "What's our mission this time?" You grit your teeth, feeling as though there are burning coals in your mouth and your body is on fire. Your voice is cracked.

t_ch: 「接住那颗星星。」她仰头，见星光自全周天驾驶舱外流溢，落入海平面之下，被波浪揉碎。你闷哼一声，想不通她为何如此镇定。
t_en: "Catch that star." She looks up at the light of the star that glides downward outside the cockpit and falls into the ocean, where it is crushed by the waves. You let out a muffled grunt, unable to figure out why she appears so calm.

t_ch: 「你在害怕。」她向你伸出手。你发现自己的手臂没有应势而动。看来是她将连接切断了。你思忖着，又见她勾动四指。「来吧，」她很沉静，「像以前那样。」
t_en: "You're scared." She extends her arm in your direction, but your arm does not move in response. It seems like she has cut off the connection. While you are deep in your thoughts, she beckons you over. "Come on," she invites in a serene tone, "let's do it like the old days."

t_ch: 你沉默地伸出手。她将指尖温柔地置入你的手心，卷起你的手指，收拢。她真的好用力，你想，一如既往。可她的身躯明明如此瘦小……
t_en: You reach out your hand silently. She gently laces your fingers together and holds your hand in a tight grip as usual. She is strong despite her slender and small body...

t_ch: 舱外的海洋依旧闪烁着星星点点的光芒。你下意识地握紧与莉亚相牵的手，以免那星光从指缝间悄悄溜走。
t_en: The stars are still sparkling on the ocean outside the cockpit. Instinctively, you tighten your hold on Leah's hand to prevent the starlight from slipping through your fingers.

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：赎罪之人。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "The redeeming man."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 你在陡峭的冰面上艰难地攀行。寒风凛冽，不知疲倦，如尖刀般切削着胆敢存在于这片高空中的一切。
t_en: You clamber along the steep slope covered in ice. The icy gale is tirelessly assaulting all beings that dare to exist at this height like a sharp blade.

t_ch: 你发觉手指几乎要被低温斫断，尽管你已穿戴好整套公司配发的尖端户外装备。但老实说，你不在乎——因为你很清楚：这荒诞的赎罪之旅即将迎来终点。
t_en: Although you are wearing a full set of advanced outdoor equipment prepared by the IPC, you realize your fingers are almost broken because of the cold. You are not bothered by it, as you are very clear that this outrageous journey of redemption is about to come to an end.

t_ch: 你已经被那帮未开化的野蛮人训诫了无数次：「根据希斯拉达恩部落合邦的古老刑罚，任何忤逆『最高贵者』的不法之徒都将被勒令前往赎罪——
t_en: Those uncivilized barbarians have admonished you countless times. "According to the ancient laws of the united tribes of Hithradahn, any who disobeys the Noblest will be ordered to redeem their sins..."

t_ch: 「那恶人将独自攀登皮格马利翁灵峰，前往最高处拓得变幻莫测的预言碑铭；若碑铭宣称赦免此人，那么他在诸位酋长的见证下重获清白！」
t_en: "The wrongdoer will climb Mount Pygmalion alone and reach the peak, where they must rub a copy of the stele's prophetic inscription. If the wrongdoer is pardoned by the inscription, then their name will be cleared in the presence of the chiefs!"

t_ch: 哈…快到了，就快到了！天空变得澄澈，冽风渐弱…你紧咬牙关，试图调动早已因失温几近坏死的臂膀，向上挥下至关重要的一镐——
t_en: Huff... almost there! The sky has cleared up, and the gale is turning into a breeze... With your teeth clenched, you try moving your hypothermia-damaged shoulder to swing the pickax for that one crucial move...

t_ch: 终于！踏上久违的大地，意识到终点已至，你不禁露出满足的笑容——尽管这笑容因僵死的面部肌肉未能落到实处。接下来，只要找到那该死的碑铭…！
t_en: Finally! Stepping on the long-awaited ground, you realize you have reached the endpoint. A smile of satisfaction crawls up your face without you realizing it, though it is not as wide as you want it to be because of your stiff facial muscles. Now, you just need to look for the darn stele...!

t_ch: …可这里哪有什么碑铭？茫茫雪甸中，不过一尊蜷曲的冰封躯体罢了！冰中之人装备齐整，因酷寒凝固的面庞，一对惊惧的眸子自旷古向你投来绝望的哀嚎……
t_en: ...But there is no stele. Instead, only a frozen curled-up body can be seen on the vast snowy landscape! The person is well-equipped, and their face is frozen due to the cold temperature. Their eyes, filled with fear, cast their howls of despair at you across millennia of time...

t_ch: ——毫无疑问地，此人正是你自己。你倒吸一口冷气，凄厉地失声，栽在坚实的雪地上，将那*可憎*的冰雕撞碎…随后便被山峰彻底夺去了知觉。
t_en: ...Undoubtedly, the frozen person is you. You gasp in shock and cry soundlessly and bitterly before collapsing on the snow and ramming yourself into the ABOMINABLE ice sculpture... Then, the mountain robs you of your consciousness.

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：理性的溃败。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "The fall of rationality."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 你的视野被数道细长且冰冷的铁栅分割。凭借指尖触感，你才确认那铁栅究竟为何物——一只精致的鸟笼——这是月桂冠系首脑「哲人王」才可拥有的冠冕。
t_en: Your field of vision is split by long, narrow, and cold iron bars. Based on what you feel with your fingertips, you confirm that the iron bars are a part of an exquisite cage — the crown that exclusively belongs to the Philosopher King, the head of the Laurel Wreath Galaxy.

t_ch: 你环顾四周，发现自己正身处于一环形剧场当中。你的脑中十分清楚，此地无疑是哲学家联合的议事集会所，星系的中心，沉思者柱廊。
t_en: You look around and realize you are in an amphitheater. You are very sure that this is the gathering place of the parliament of the Philosopher Union. It is the center of the galaxy, the colonnade of the contemplators.

t_ch: 在那大理石柱下列席的本该是来自星系各地最智慧的头脑，可如今只充满无数嚣叫的钢铁身躯。他们高举铁拳，激昂，愤懑，用电流模拟刺耳嘈杂的通用语：
t_en: The seats below the marble pillars were supposed to be filled by the most brilliant minds of the star system, but are now occupied by countless screaming steel bodies. They raise their metallic fists in the air indignantly and use electrical currents to noisily and shrilly imitate the common tongue:

t_ch: 「消灭有机生命！」、「消灭奥勒里乌斯！」、「帝皇万岁！」…如此看来，奥勒里乌斯一定是你的名字，而这也确实与你的记忆完全吻合。
t_en: "Eliminate all organic lifeforms!" "Destroy Aurelius!" "Long live the emperor!" It seems like Aurelius is your name, and it matches what you remember.

t_ch: 你很清楚这诸多硅基生命为何如此愤怒，自己又如何成为众矢之的——根据反有机方程，统治星系的哲学家联合必将倾覆于机械帝国的铁蹄下——就是这么简单。
t_en: You are well aware of what has triggered the wrath of these silicon-based lifeforms and why you have become the target of public condemnation. According to the Anti-Organic Equation, the Philosopher Union that rules the star system will certainly be destroyed by the Machine Empire — it is as simple as that.

t_ch: 它们想要羞辱你，这位领导星系所有生灵的、唯一的代表！尽管他们已经将这星系中的许多星球毁灭殆尽，但它们仍不知足——
t_en: They want to humiliate you, the sole representative and leader of all living beings in the star system! Although they have wiped out many planets in the star system, they are still not satisfied...

t_ch: 它们判令你有罪；它们决心以哲人的法律处决你，逼迫你饮下面前的毒药…它们乐于见证你的陨落，见证有机生命的陨落，见证哲学家联合与理性的陨落！
t_en: They convict and punish you according to the law of Philosophers, forcing you to drink the poison before you... They are happy to witness your death and the demise of organic lifeforms, the Philosopher Union, and rationality!

t_ch: 你踏步向前，高举充盈剧毒的金杯。「异质的诸位生命啊，请务必铭记！」你扬起头颅环视：「思想不会*畏惧*死亡——思想绝不会死亡！」
t_en: You step forward and lift the golden cup that contains lethal poison. "Foreign-based lifeforms, remember this!" You hold your head high and look around. "Thoughts do not fear DEATH. Thoughts will never die!"

t_ch: 「我，奥勒里乌斯，笼中的窥视者，痴愚的牧人，一切哲人之王，在此向各位致敬！」你振臂，「因各位将在这神圣柱廊的注视下欢庆胜利，禀受真正的理性！」
t_en: "I, Aurelius, the watcher in the cage, the foolish shepherd, and the king of all Philosophers, hail you all!" You raise your arms. "You will celebrate your victory under the gaze of this sacred colonnade, and be endowed with true rationality!"

t_ch: 狂热的音浪淹没了你的话语。你大笑，将鸩毒一饮而尽。
t_en: Your words are drowned by the maddening cacophony. You roar with laughter, and down the cup of poison in one go.

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：宴席的尾声。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "The end of a banquet."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 你见那方桌长达十余米，自近处通贯狭长的廊厅。你手执精美刀叉，光可鉴人的银器表面尚且挂着鲜甜的酱汁。而更远些的地方则是各类寰宇珍味，不一而足。
t_en: You can see the long table stretching out for more than ten meters, traversing the entirety of the long and narrow hall. You are holding a beautiful set of cutlery in your hands, with their shiny silvery surfaces still dripping with delicious sauce. All types of gourmet food imaginable are served on the side further away from you.

t_ch: 你望向方桌左边。离你最近的是提乌德里克，那是家族中的长子，最为能征善战。若没有他，你无法想象这偌大的王国如何在军团的猛攻下幸存。
t_en: You turn to the left side of the table. Theuderic, who sits nearest to you, is the eldest son of the family and a most valiant soldier. Without him, you cannot imagine how this kingdom could have survived the relentless attacks from the Legion.

t_ch: 更向后一席者是克洛多梅尔，虽为次子却统辖最辽阔的星区。那是诸多无人敢于染指的死寂星球，在他治下却变得欣欣向荣。他是最精明的，你对他抱以厚望。
t_en: Next to Theuderic is Chlodomer, your second son but the ruler of the largest stellar space. Silent and barren planets where people feared to tread are transformed into flourishing places under his leadership. You have high hopes for him, as he is the most crafty among your children.

t_ch: 你望向方桌右边。希尔德贝尼娅是你最宠爱的长女。她是一名无畏的战士，有着如火焰般炽烈的鲜红卷发。临近星系的将领们都畏惧地称她为「王国的烈焰」。
t_en: Then, you look to the right side of the table. Hildebenia is your eldest daughter and your favorite. She is a fearless warrior with flaming red curls in her hair. Generals in neighboring star systems fearfully call her "The Kingdom's Flame."

t_ch: 在其身后的则是从不愿以真面目示人的克洛妲尼娅。她是王国中最灵敏的头脑，自幕帘的阴影中谨慎地驾驭着王国治下的一切星球。她是你最得力的顾问。
t_en: The one next to Hildebenia is Chlodania, who never reveals her face in public. With the brightest mind in the kingdom, she carefully manages from the shadows all the planets under the kingdom's rule. She is your most competent adviser.

t_ch: ——如果有他们在，那么这宴席必将充满欢声笑语。然而，如今这破败的廊厅中却只剩下无边的寂静在回荡。
t_en: ...If they were here, the banquet would be a lively and joyful occasion. But now, there is nothing but silence in this run-down hall.

t_ch: 你只是闭眼，将又一块鲜美的高里亚驼牛肉送进麻痹的口腔。你深陷回忆。你想起提乌德里克前不久便与王国的星舰一同殉难……
t_en: You can only close your eyes as you put a chunk of delectable Gorya beef into your numb mouth, drowning in memories. You remember Theuderic had passed away not long ago with one of the kingdom's starships...
